name: dedupe (Machine Learning Record Deduplication)

performance.notes: Active learning training fast; blocking + clustering can be slow for >1M records

agent_readiness.notes: Machine learning-based record deduplication. import dedupe. fields = [{'field': 'Name', 'type': 'String'}, {'field': 'Address', 'type': 'String'}]. deduper = dedupe.Dedupe(fields). deduper.prepare_training(data). dedupe.console_label(deduper). deduper.train(). deduper.write_settings(settings_file). clusters = deduper.partition(data, threshold=0.5). Blocking: reduces O(n^2) comparisons to feasible set. Active learning: human labels improve model. gazetteer.Gazetteer for record linkage against canonical list. RecordLink for linking two datasets.

gotchas: Requires active learning training (interactive human labeling) for good results
Training settings must be saved and reloaded — not deterministic without saved model
Blocking predicates must be appropriate for data — bad blocking = missed duplicates
Not for large scale (>10M records) without custom blocking
best_when: Entity deduplication with ML where manual training labels are available
Deduplicating company, person, or address records
When simple fuzzy matching (rapidfuzz) doesn't produce good enough clusters
Record linkage between two datasets (RecordLink class)
avoid_when: No time for active learning training
summary: dedupe provides ML-based record deduplication with active learning. Dedupe(fields). prepare_training(data). console_label() for interactive training. deduper.train(). partition(data, threshold=0.5). RecordLink for cross-dataset matching. Gazetteer for canonical-list linkage. MIT by DataMade. Best when ML training labels improve on simple fuzzy matching. Requires interactive training. Not for >10M records.
